Apologies, I missed it there 

1) keeping `Parameter is not declared` is a great option but the error should also make the user aware that he can use the --implicit-typing option with the parameter.  

2) the error `Parameter `" + sym + "` has no IMPLICIT Type` arises when one uses both --implicit-typing option and implicit none together.
 
for example, the below code
`program test_implicit_typing_disabled
    implicit none
    parameter (a = 3.14)
end program
`

> 
> Is this the right default choice? I think so, since implicit typing should not be used for new codes. And for old codes, typically you are aware of this. Where we should suggest it is for "implicit real ..." implicit statements. But just for not declaring a variable I think we should not, since the user should declare it. >
